skill.name: software-copyright-materials
skill.description: Generate guided Chinese software copyright application materials from a real project. Use this skill when the user asks for 软件著作权, 软著申请资料, 软著代码材料, 操作手册, 申请表信息, or wants Word/TXT materials for software copyright registration. The workflow analyzes the imported project, extracts real source code, creates Markdown drafts for user confirmation, then uses bundled DOCX tooling to produce final Word documents and TXT.
skill.summary: Generate Chinese software copyright registration documents (Word/TXT) from real project source code with guided manual drafting, code extraction, and DOCX output.
skill.author: Fokkyp

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 3200 potential issues (risk score 100/100) across 93 files, all of which were evaluated as false positives from overly aggressive pattern matching. The 'external_commands' detections in SKILL.md and .md references are markdown code fences documenting workflow commands for the AI model, not executable code. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections in yaml/md/xml files are substring matches (e.g., 'des' in 'description', 'default' in 'default_prompt'). 'subprocess.run' calls in Python scripts run hardcoded commands (pandoc, dotnet --version) for document generation and environment checking, with no user input injection vectors. 'Hardcoded URL' findings in XML/XSD files are XML namespace URIs (schemas.openxmlformats.org) required for document format validation. The skill is a legitimate software copyright materials generation tool for Chinese copyright registration. No credential exfiltration, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found. Risk factors (scripts, external_commands, filesystem, network) reflect legitimate tool usage.

medium_findings.title: Python subprocess calls in build scripts
medium_findings.description: The skill uses subprocess.run() to call pandoc for document conversion and bash scripts for environment checking. All commands use hardcoded arguments with no user input injection vectors. The commands (pandoc, dotnet --version, bash env_check.sh) are legitimate document generation tooling.

medium_findings.title: Bundled vendor scripts with sudo and system access
medium_findings.description: The vendor/docx-toolkit/scripts/setup.sh and env_check.sh contain sudo commands and system-level operations for installing .NET SDK dependencies. These are part of an optional setup workflow that explicitly requires user consent before execution.

low_findings.title: High volume of false positive static detections
low_findings.description: The static analyzer produced 3200 findings, nearly all false positives: backtick detection in .md files (markdown code fences), 'weak crypto' substring matches in yaml/xml (e.g., 'des' in 'description'), URL matches from XML namespace URIs, and heuristic entropy spikes from Chinese UTF-8 text.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run with external tools
dangerous_patterns.description: Scripts use subprocess.run() to invoke pandoc (document conversion), dotnet CLI (environment check), and bash (setup/env scripts). All commands use hardcoded arguments. No shell=True or user-input injection vectors exist.

dangerous_patterns.title: Vendor setup script with privilege escalation
dangerous_patterns.description: vendor/docx-toolkit/scripts/setup.sh uses sudo for .NET SDK and LibreOffice installation. This is an optional dependency setup that requires explicit user opt-in via the environment gating stage.

content.user_title: Generate Chinese Software Copyright Application Materials
content.value_statement: Preparing Chinese software copyright registration documents is time-consuming and requires extracting real source code, writing operation manuals, and filling application forms correctly. This skill automates the workflow by analyzing your project, extracting authenticated source code, creating reviewable Markdown drafts, and generating final Word and TXT documents ready for submission.
actual_capabilities: Analyze project structure to identify programming language, framework, entry points, and source files
Extract real source code (not AI-generated) from confirmed project files for code material documents
Generate Markdown drafts for business understanding, application form, code materials, and operation manual
Produce final DOCX and TXT files using built-in vendor/docx-toolkit with proper formatting
Guide users through mandatory confirmation gates for software name, version, fields, and code selection
Support Chrome DevTools MCP, Codex Computer Use, or user-supplied screenshots for manual illustrations
limitations: Only supports Chinese software copyright registration workflow (not applicable to other jurisdictions)
Requires user confirmation at multiple gating stages — not fully autonomous
Full DOCX OpenXML features require .NET SDK installation; falls back to basic DOCX otherwise
Code extraction capped at 60 pages (30+30) per application standards; does not generate full codebase dumps

use_cases.title: Software Developer Preparing Copyright Filing
use_cases.description: A developer with a completed project needs to prepare copyright registration materials. The skill analyzes the project, extracts the most representative source code, and generates all required Word and TXT documents with proper formatting.

use_cases.title: Legal Assistant Processing Multiple Registrations
use_cases.description: A legal assistant handling copyright filings for multiple software products uses the skill to standardize document generation, ensuring consistent formatting and complete field coverage across all applications.

use_cases.title: Project Manager Reviewing Application Drafts
use_cases.description: A project manager needs to review and confirm copyright application details before submission. The skill generates human-readable Markdown drafts for each stage, allowing non-technical stakeholders to verify business descriptions and field accuracy.

prompt_templates.title: Start full copyright material generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate Chinese software copyright application materials for the project in the current directory.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a completed project and want to create all copyright application documents

prompt_templates.title: Only generate code materials
prompt_templates.prompt: I need code material documents for copyright registration. Analyze my project first, propose which source files to extract, and let me confirm before generating the DOCX files.
prompt_templates.scenario: You only need the code materials portion of the application

prompt_templates.title: Generate with business context emphasis
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate copyright application materials for this project. Pay special attention to understanding the business domain, target users, and core features before drafting the operation manual. The software is a web-based e-commerce platform targeting small business owners.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want the AI to deeply understand your software's business context before generating documents

prompt_templates.title: Review and fix existing drafts
prompt_templates.prompt: I already have copyright application drafts in the 'draft' folder. Please review the business understanding, application form fields, and code selection. Fix any issues, standardize the software name across all documents, and then generate the final DOCX outputs.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have existing draft materials that need review and finalization

output_examples.input: Generate copyright materials for a React e-commerce project
output: Markdown drafts folder with business understanding, application form fields, code candidate list, and operation manual
Final DOCX files with formatted code materials (first 30 pages and last 30 pages)
Application form TXT with all confirmed fields
Operation manual DOCX with screenshots and proper chapter structure

output_examples.input: Confirm code selection and generate final documents
output: Code extraction manifest listing each selected source file with line ranges and reasons
Professionally formatted DOCX with page headers showing confirmed software name and version
Generation report summarizing all outputs and validation checks

best_practices: Confirm the software full name and version number explicitly before generating final documents — these appear in file names, page headers, and throughout the manual
Review the code file selection carefully to ensure the most representative source files are included, not auto-generated or test files
Read through the business understanding draft before approving it — this becomes the foundation for both the application form and operation manual content
anti_patterns: Do not skip the user confirmation gates — the workflow requires explicit approval at each stage before proceeding to the next
Do not allow AI to fabricate code content — all code materials must be extracted from real project source files confirmed in the code selection
Do not use HTML comments or invisible markers for screenshot placeholders — use visible text blocks that appear in the final DOCX output

faq.question: What types of projects does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill works with any project that has source code files. It has built-in support for frontend frameworks (React, Vue, Next.js), backend services (Node.js, Python), and recognizes common project structures. It analyzes package.json, source directories, and entry points automatically.

faq.question: Does this generate AI-fabricated code for the copyright materials?
faq.answer: No. The skill only extracts real source code from your confirmed project files. Code materials are always sourced from actual project source code. The code file selection stage lets you review and approve exactly which files and line ranges to include.

faq.question: What if my project has fewer than 60 pages of source code?
faq.answer: The skill first tries to select the most representative 60 pages from your source files. If your project has fewer than 60 pages of source code, it generates a single complete code document instead of splitting into first 30 and last 30 pages.

faq.question: Do I need to install additional software to use this skill?
faq.answer: Basic Markdown and TXT output works without any additional software. For full DOCX generation with proper OpenXML formatting, you need .NET SDK. The skill checks your environment at startup and offers options to install dependencies or continue with basic output.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-Chinese copyright registrations?
faq.answer: This skill is specifically designed for the Chinese software copyright registration process (软件著作权登记). The document formats, field requirements, and output structures follow Chinese copyright office standards and may not apply to other jurisdictions.

faq.question: How does the screenshot feature work?
faq.answer: The skill supports three methods: Chrome DevTools MCP for web app screenshots, Codex Computer Use for desktop application screenshots, or you can provide your own screenshots by placing them in a designated folder. You can also skip screenshots entirely and the manual will include visible placeholder markers.
